title: Literary Devices Essentials
description: Metaphor, simile, personification, alliteration, irony — the building blocks of literature.
summary_pt_br: Recursos literários: metáfora, símile, personificação, aliteração, ironia.

scenes.subtitle_en: Metaphor, simile, personification, alliteration, irony — the building blocks of literature.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Recursos literários: metáfora, símile, personificação, aliteração, ironia.

scenes.text_en: Metaphor, simile, personification, alliteration, irony — the building blocks of literature. Today's focus: literary devices essentials. Time is a river; it never flows backward.
scenes.text_pt: Recursos literários: metáfora, símile, personificação, aliteração, ironia. Foco de hoje: literary devices essentials. O tempo é um rio; nunca volta.

scenes.title: Literary Devices Essentials
scenes.body_en: Metaphor: direct comparison ('time is a river'). Simile: 'like' or 'as' ('quiet as stone'). Personification: human traits to non-human. Irony: the gap between expectation and reality.
scenes.body_pt: Recursos literários: metáfora, símile, personificação, aliteração, ironia.

examples.en: Time is a river; it never flows backward.
examples.pt: O tempo é um rio; nunca volta.

examples.en: She was as quiet as stone when she heard the news.
examples.pt: Ela ficou quieta como pedra ao ouvir a notícia.

examples.en: The wind whispered secrets through the trees.
examples.pt: O vento sussurrava segredos pelas árvores.

exercise.question: Complete: "Time is a river; it never flows ___."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Time is a river; it never flows backward."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O tempo é um rio; nunca volta.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
She was as quiet as stone when she heard the news.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "She was as quiet as stone when she heard the news."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ela ficou quieta como pedra ao ouvir a notícia.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Literary Devices Essentials — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre literary devices essentials. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Use a metaphor to describe grief.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Grief is an ocean that remembers every shore.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Joy is like sunlight on wet leaves.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The wind whispered stories through the window.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Give me one line of alliteration.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Use irony in a short sentence.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The fire station burned down last night.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Literature is grammar dressed for the stars.

scenes.title: The poetry workshop
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write about a rainy afternoon using at least three literary devices.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Dona Lúcia read a short poem aloud, then asked the students to find every device hidden inside the lines.

paragraphs.speaker: Lúcia
paragraphs.en: Time is a river, she wrote — a metaphor that even a child can feel in the heart first, mind second.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And the line 'the wind whispered through the trees' is personification, right?

paragraphs.speaker: Lúcia
paragraphs.en: Yes — and the repeated S sounds give it a gentle alliteration that matches the image.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: The irony of the fire station burning down taught me more about irony than any textbook.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Literary devices, Lúcia believed, are not decoration — they are the quiet bones of every memorable sentence.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Literary Devices Essentials". Metaphor, simile, personification, alliteration, irony — the building blocks of literature. Time is a river; it never flows backward. She was as quiet as stone when she heard the news. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Literary Devices Essentials". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Literary Devices Essentials".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
